# Generated by openai (gpt-3.5-turbo)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import {
  validateRequest,
  json,
  serve,
  Response,
  Router,
  Status,
} from "https://deno.land/x/deploy@v1.4.1";

const { KV } = Deno;
const db = new KV("data");

const router = new Router();

router.get("/search", async (req) => {
  const userId = req.url.split("/")[2];
  const queryParams = req.url.searchParams;

  const filterParam1 = queryParams.get("filter1");
  const filterParam2 = queryParams.get("filter2");
  // Add more filter params as needed

  const result1 = await db.get(["users", userId, filterParam1]);
  const result2 = await db.get(["users", userId, filterParam2]);
  // Lookup more filter params as needed

  const data = {
    result1: result1 ? await result1.text() : null,
    result2: result2 ? await result2.text() : null,
    // Add more results as needed
  };

  return new Response(JSON.stringify(data), {
    headers: { "content-type": "application/json" },
    status: Status.OK,
  });
});

addEventListener("fetch", (event) => {
  const { request } = event;
  const response = router.handle(request);
  event.respondWith(response);
});
